scope.context_note: Volume II section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Arithmetic Operations and Relations', section 'Field And Ordered Field Laws'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.
review_instructions.state: Use reorder/state/working-delta.json as cumulative accepted state across all batches.
review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: Field Zero Product
nodes.statement_display:
In a field,
\[
a\cdot b=0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
a=0\ \text{or}\ b=0.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In a field,
\[
a\cdot b=0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
a=0\ \text{or}\ b=0.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Field
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

direct_dependencies.title: No Zero Divisors
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In an integral domain, \[ a\cdot b=0 \Longrightarrow a=0 \lor b=0. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Integral Domain
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{integral domain} is a commutative ring with $0\ne1$ and no zero divisors.

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutative Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: No Zero Divisors
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an integral domain, \[ a\cdot b=0 \Longrightarrow a=0 \lor b=0. \]

nodes.title: Group Cancellation
nodes.statement_display:
In a group, cancellation holds on both sides:
\[
a\ast c=b\ast c\Longrightarrow a=b
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
c\ast a=c\ast b\Longrightarrow a=b.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In a group, cancellation holds on both sides:
\[
a\ast c=b\ast c\Longrightarrow a=b
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
c\ast a=c\ast b\Longrightarrow a=b.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

ancestor_tree.title: Group
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

dependencies.title: Monoid
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

dependencies.title: Semigroup
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

dependencies.title: Associativity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Binary Closure
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

dependencies.title: Function

nodes.title: Field Multiplicative Cancellation
nodes.statement_display:
In a field,
\[
c\ne0\land a\cdot c=b\cdot c
\Longrightarrow
a=b.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In a field,
\[
c\ne0\land a\cdot c=b\cdot c
\Longrightarrow
a=b.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Field
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

direct_dependencies.title: Group Cancellation
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, cancellation holds on both sides: \[ a\ast c=b\ast c\Longrightarrow a=b \qquad\text{and}\qquad c\ast a=c\ast b\Longrightarrow a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutative Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Group Cancellation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, cancellation holds on both sides: \[ a\ast c=b\ast c\Longrightarrow a=b \qquad\text{and}\qquad c\ast a=c\ast b\Longrightarrow a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

direct_dependencies.title: Group
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

direct_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function

transitive_dependencies.title: Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

dependencies.title: Left Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Right Identity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

ancestor_tree.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

dependencies.title: Associativity
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

nodes.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
nodes.statement_display: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.
nodes.statement_tex: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

direct_dependencies.title: Field
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutative Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

nodes.title: Field Integer Power
nodes.statement_display:
Let $F$ be a field. For $a\in F$, powers $a^n$ with $n\in\mathbb{W}$ are
defined recursively. If $a\ne0$ and $n\in\mathbb{Z}$ is negative, define
\[
a^n:=(a^{-1})^{-n}.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $F$ be a field. For $a\in F$, powers $a^n$ with $n\in\mathbb{W}$ are
defined recursively. If $a\ne0$ and $n\in\mathbb{Z}$ is negative, define
\[
a^n:=(a^{-1})^{-n}.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Field
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

direct_dependencies.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Group Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, each element has a unique inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

nodes.title: Field Exponent Addition
nodes.statement_display:
In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $m,n\in\mathbb{Z}$, then
\[
a^{m+n}=a^m\cdot a^n.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $m,n\in\mathbb{Z}$, then
\[
a^{m+n}=a^m\cdot a^n.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Field Integer Power
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $F$ be a field. For $a\in F$, powers $a^n$ with $n\in\mathbb{W}$ are defined recursively. If $a\ne0$ and $n\in\mathbb{Z}$ is negative, define \[ a^n:=(a^{-1})^{-n}. \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Group Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, each element has a unique inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

ancestor_tree.title: Field Integer Power
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $F$ be a field. For $a\in F$, powers $a^n$ with $n\in\mathbb{W}$ are defined recursively. If $a\ne0$ and $n\in\mathbb{Z}$ is negative, define \[ a^n:=(a^{-1})^{-n}. \]

direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.title: Binary Closure
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

dependencies.title: Left Inverse

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutative Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Group

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, each element has a unique inverse.

nodes.title: Field Inverse of Product
nodes.statement_display:
In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $b\ne0$, then
\[
(a\cdot b)^{-1}=a^{-1}\cdot b^{-1}.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:

(a\cdot b)^{-1}=a^{-1}\cdot b^{-1}.
\]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Zero Product
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, \[ a\cdot b=0 \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a=0\ \text{or}\ b=0. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Group Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, each element has a unique inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: No Zero Divisors
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an integral domain, \[ a\cdot b=0 \Longrightarrow a=0 \lor b=0. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

nodes.title: Generic Field Laws
nodes.statement_display:
Every field satisfies the zero-product law, cancellation laws, uniqueness of
inverses, inverse laws, ring sign laws, and integer-exponent laws listed in
this topic.
nodes.statement_tex:
Every field satisfies the zero-product law, cancellation laws, uniqueness of
inverses, inverse laws, ring sign laws, and integer-exponent laws listed in
this topic.

direct_dependencies.title: Field Exponent Addition
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $m,n\in\mathbb{Z}$, then \[ a^{m+n}=a^m\cdot a^n. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Field Inverse of Product
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $b\ne0$, then \[ (a\cdot b)^{-1}=a^{-1}\cdot b^{-1}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Field Multiplicative Cancellation
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, \[ c\ne0\land a\cdot c=b\cdot c \Longrightarrow a=b. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Field Zero Product
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, \[ a\cdot b=0 \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a=0\ \text{or}\ b=0. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Integer Power
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $F$ be a field. For $a\in F$, powers $a^n$ with $n\in\mathbb{W}$ are defined recursively. If $a\ne0$ and $n\in\mathbb{Z}$ is negative, define \[ a^n:=(a^{-1})^{-n}. \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Exponent Addition
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $m,n\in\mathbb{Z}$, then \[ a^{m+n}=a^m\cdot a^n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Inverse of Product
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $b\ne0$, then \[ (a\cdot b)^{-1}=a^{-1}\cdot b^{-1}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Multiplicative Cancellation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, \[ c\ne0\land a\cdot c=b\cdot c \Longrightarrow a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Zero Product
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, \[ a\cdot b=0 \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a=0\ \text{or}\ b=0. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Group Cancellation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, cancellation holds on both sides: \[ a\ast c=b\ast c\Longrightarrow a=b \qquad\text{and}\qquad c\ast a=c\ast b\Longrightarrow a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Group Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, each element has a unique inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: No Zero Divisors
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an integral domain, \[ a\cdot b=0 \Longrightarrow a=0 \lor b=0. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.

ancestor_tree.title: Field Exponent Addition
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $m,n\in\mathbb{Z}$, then \[ a^{m+n}=a^m\cdot a^n. \]

dependencies.title: Field Integer Power
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $F$ be a field. For $a\in F$, powers $a^n$ with $n\in\mathbb{W}$ are defined recursively. If $a\ne0$ and $n\in\mathbb{Z}$ is negative, define \[ a^n:=(a^{-1})^{-n}. \]

nodes.title: Field Power of Power
nodes.statement_display:
In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $m,n\in\mathbb{Z}$, then
\[
(a^m)^n=a^{m\cdot n}.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
In a field, if $a\ne0$ and $m,n\in\mathbb{Z}$, then
\[
(a^m)^n=a^{m\cdot n}.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Field Integer Power
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $F$ be a field. For $a\in F$, powers $a^n$ with $n\in\mathbb{W}$ are defined recursively. If $a\ne0$ and $n\in\mathbb{Z}$ is negative, define \[ a^n:=(a^{-1})^{-n}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left and Right Inverses Coincide
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, a left inverse and a right inverse of the same element are equal.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a field, each nonzero element has a unique multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Group Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In a group, each element has a unique inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, inverses are unique.
